rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Celestine,

I know this has been a frustrating few weeks, and I really appreciate you sending all those photos and forwarding both order confirmations. I have been going through everything carefully so I can give you a clear answer instead of a bunch of back-and-forth.

For order R-8801, delivered April 18 and reported April 22, the cracked mixing bowl set qualifies within the 5-day damage window. I can process a partial refund of $29.40 for the mixing bowl set only. It is not a refund for the full order because the rest of the items in R-8801 — the silicone tray set and the dish rack — arrived undamaged per your own photos, so those are not covered.

For order R-8854, the blender jar with a cracked lid was reported May 3, but the delivery scan shows May 1. Since the damage was reported outside the 5-day window, my options are more limited. What I can do is send one replacement blender lid at no cost as a goodwill gesture, but I cannot process a cash refund for the lid from R-8854.

To move both forward, please send me your current shipping address by May 14 so I can queue the replacement lid. I also need you to confirm in this thread that you are okay with the $29.40 partial refund path for R-8801 instead of a full order refund. Once I have both confirmations, I will queue the refund and the replacement shipment at the same time. If the $29.40 does not show up within five business days after I process it, please reach back out to me directly.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not change the $59.00 credit amount or the $30.00 post-credit balance.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Bertrand,

I’ve reviewed your billing dispute, which involves three invoices.

Invoice INV-2210 for $148.00 covers March 1–31, when your team was on the Standard plan. Since the service was used for the full billing period without mid-month changes, I cannot issue a refund for that invoice.

Invoice INV-2291 for $148.00 covers April 1–30. Your downgrade to Basic was processed on April 1, but the system charged the Standard rate because the plan switch didn’t propagate before the invoice ran. This was a billing error, so I can issue a $59.00 credit—the difference between the $148.00 Standard charge and the $89.00 Basic rate you should have been charged. If you confirm by June 5, I’ll apply that credit to your next invoice, INV-2361.

Invoice INV-2361 is the current open invoice for $89.00, covering May 1–31. It’s correct. After the $59.00 credit, the balance would be $30.00. If I don’t hear back by June 5, the credit window for INV-2291 closes and the full $89.00 remains due.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for your time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. To make it easy to compare, here are the three options based on quote Q-5544, all expiring June 20.

The base quote covers 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, totaling $1,100 per month. This includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support.

Add-on A adds API access for a flat $300 per month, keeping the seat price at $55. The monthly total becomes $1,400. Add-on B is a dedicated onboarding program with a one-time fee of $1,800 for four structured sessions. It can be combined with Add-on A, resulting in a $1,400 monthly cost plus the one-time $1,800.

The custom dashboard integration mentioned on Friday is not part of any of these options. Adding it would require a separate scoping conversation and a new quote—I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

If you’d like to proceed with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I’ll send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to explore the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I’ll start that process separately.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say students entering grade 3 in the fall are eligible.
Do not say partial payment is accepted or that a spot can be held without full payment.

rows.RewrittenText:
Hello Ridgemont families,

We’re excited to share the Summer Enrichment Program details for 2026. It runs July 7 through July 25 in Room 104 on the Ridgemont East campus. The program is open to current Ridgemont students in grades 3 through 6—students entering grade 3 in the fall are not eligible.

Space is limited to 24 students. If we get more than 24 complete applications, we’ll start a waitlist in the order we receive them. The interest list from last year doesn’t guarantee a spot, so every family needs to submit a new application this year.

The fee is $185 per student, due with the application. Both must reach the main office by Friday, June 13 at 4 p.m. You can get Form SEP-2026 at the main office window or on the school website. We can’t accept partial payment or hold a spot without payment. If your child ends up on the waitlist, we’ll keep the $185 until a spot opens or the deadline passes—then we’ll return it if no spot becomes available.

Questions? Email enrichment@example.com or stop by the main office Tuesday through Thursday, 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.

Best,

Hi Vivienne,

I reviewed order ORD-29447 and the return you started on June 2. Each item is at a different stage, so here’s a clear breakdown.

Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3, but we haven’t received the package yet. Once it arrives, I’ll process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3–5 business days.

Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): We received it on June 5, confirmed it’s undamaged and unused, and issued the $22.50 refund on June 6. It should appear on your statement within 2 business days.

Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You mentioned it arrived cracked. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send it by June 10 at noon, or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can’t issue the carafe refund until we physically receive it, and I can’t send the replacement lid without the photo. Everything else is in progress.

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not interpret or describe the contents of the imaging records.

Do not accept a paper form photo as an alternative to the portal submission.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Gwendolyn,

I'm following up on your request to have Dr. Okafor's office receive a copy of your imaging records from your April 18 appointment. We can't release records without your signed authorization, so here are the steps to get this moving.

1. Log in to the patient portal at portal.example.org and go to "My Records" then "Release Authorization."
2. Complete the Release of Medical Records form. Make sure you select Dr. Okafor's practice as the recipient and check the box for "Diagnostic Imaging" under record type.
3. Sign and submit the form inside the portal. Please don't email a photo of a paper form — the portal version is the only one our records team will accept.

Once the signed form is received, our records team typically processes release requests within 5 business days. If you haven't heard anything within 7 business days of submitting the form, call the records office at ext. 3114.

I can't give you a status on the release or contact Dr. Okafor's office on your behalf until the signed authorization is in our system. If you believe this is urgent, please ask Dr. Okafor's office to mark the incoming request as urgent and fax a priority cover sheet to 555-0192.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not diagnose or label Soren behaviorally.
Do not say the check-in with Ms. Vega is disciplinary.

rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

I want to address what happened on May 15 in the classroom. During independent reading time, Soren was observed pushing another student's book off the desk, and when I asked him to return to his seat, he said he did not care and turned away. I am not labeling Soren or suggesting there is something wrong with him—I am sharing what I observed so we can figure out together what support looks like going forward.

Since May 15, I have checked in with Soren every morning during arrival, and he has been calm and engaged in those moments. I have also put Soren's name on Ms. Vega's list for a voluntary check-in, which is not a disciplinary step. Ms. Vega is our school counselor and works with students in exactly these situations.

I would like to meet with you to talk through this properly. I have availability on May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 during the 9 to 10 a.m. window. Please let me know which works, and we can confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

To make sure Kessler Group’s needs are documented, I submitted a formal feature priority request on May 17—reference FPR-2241. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a deeper roadmap discussion.

If you’d like that intro, just reply and I’ll set it up by the end of this week. I can’t promise custom development or a discounted renewal, but I’m here to help however I can.
